baseline_policy: Compression claims must compare against terse English, not polite or verbose English. Whole-message counts are authoritative; lexicon audits alone do not imply compression.

cases.description: The retired README/spec example must remain a regression fixture because it is larger than both verbose and terse English on certified OpenAI tokenizers.

forms.label: verbose_english
forms.text: Could you check whether the deploy of the edge function to the Supabase project succeeded, and if it failed, look at the logs and tell me the first error with a timestamp?

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check Supabase edge-function deploy status. If failed, return first log error with timestamp.

cases.description: A narrow structured-query win using compact field syntax.

forms.label: verbose_english
forms.text: Please give me the names and email addresses of all users who signed up in the last 7 days and have not yet verified their account.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: List name, email of users signed up in last 7 days with unverified accounts.

cases.description: A narrow conditional workflow win using single-token arrows.

forms.label: verbose_english
forms.text: First run the test suite. If all tests pass, build the project and deploy it to production. If any test fails, stop and report which test failed.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Run tests. If all pass: build, deploy to prod. If any fail: stop, report failing test.

forms.label: compact_form
forms.text: run tests; all pass -> build, deploy prod; any fail -> stop, report fail
